skill.name: vitepress
skill.description: Guides AI agents through building, configuring, theming, and deploying VitePress documentation sites with Vue and Markdown.
skill.summary: Build and maintain VitePress documentation sites with practical guidance for config, routing, themes, Markdown, and deployment.
skill.author: antfu

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are Markdown reference examples for VitePress usage rather than executable skill code. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, malicious network behavior, or hidden command execution was found; residual risk is limited to examples that may guide users to run normal build, deploy, fetch, or file-loading workflows.

low_findings.title: Markdown Reference Examples Trigger Command Heuristics
low_findings.description: Static command findings are false positives from Markdown examples showing standard VitePress CLI and deployment commands. These examples are not executed by the skill itself and do not include command injection behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is fenced Markdown documentation for normal VitePress, npm, and deployment commands. No executable wrapper, shell interpolation, or user-controlled command construction was found.
low_findings.recommendation: Publish with a standard note that users should review generated shell commands before running them.

low_findings.title: Legitimate Build-Time File and Network Examples
low_findings.description: Filesystem and network findings appear in VitePress data loader and dynamic route examples. They demonstrate documented build-time loading of local files or public APIs, not unauthorized data access or exfiltration by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples are scoped to user-authored VitePress loaders and use placeholder URLs or project files. I found no code that sends secrets, reads arbitrary sensitive paths, or runs automatically from the skill.
low_findings.recommendation: Keep the marketplace warning low-level and advise users to constrain loaders to intended project files and trusted APIs.

low_findings.title: Weak-Crypto and Secret Findings Are Documentation Tokens
low_findings.description: Weak cryptography and secret-key findings are false positives from terms such as Markdown file extensions, dark mode labels, and Algolia placeholder fields. No real cryptographic implementation or hardcoded credential value was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain documentation terminology and placeholder values, not a hashing algorithm or usable secret. The only API key text reviewed is a placeholder string in an Algolia configuration example.
low_findings.recommendation: No security block is needed. Encourage users to replace placeholders with their own managed credentials outside generated public examples.

low_findings.title: Dynamic Imports Are SSR Compatibility Guidance
low_findings.description: Dynamic import findings are legitimate VitePress and Vue SSR guidance for browser-only libraries. The imports use placeholder package names and are guarded by lifecycle hooks or SSR checks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The semantic context is SSR compatibility documentation, and the imports are static placeholder package names. I found no remote import URL or runtime code-loading payload.
low_findings.recommendation: Publish as low risk. Users should still review generated package imports for supply-chain trust.

content.value_statement: VitePress projects need correct configuration, routing, Markdown patterns, and deployment settings. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused guidance for building and maintaining VitePress sites.
actual_capabilities: Explains VitePress project setup, CLI commands, and common package scripts.
Guides site configuration for metadata, base paths, build options, rewrites, and Markdown settings.
Documents Markdown features, code block options, Vue usage, data loading, and dynamic routes.
Covers default theme navigation, sidebar, search, social links, footer, and edit link settings.
Provides custom theme and default theme extension guidance for Vue components and CSS variables.
Summarizes deployment patterns for GitHub Pages, Netlify, Vercel, Cloudflare Pages, GitLab Pages, Firebase, and Nginx.
limitations: The skill is based on VitePress 1.x documentation generated on 2026-01-28.
It provides guidance and examples, but it does not run builds, deploy sites, or validate hosting accounts.
It cannot guarantee compatibility with future VitePress, Vue, Node, or hosting provider changes.
Generated configuration should be reviewed against the project repository and deployment environment.

use_cases.title: Create a Documentation Site
use_cases.description: Generate a practical VitePress structure with configuration, navigation, sidebar, and starter Markdown pages.
use_cases.target_user: Developer starting product or library docs

use_cases.title: Improve Existing Docs
use_cases.description: Review routing, Markdown usage, code blocks, theme settings, and content organization for an existing site.
use_cases.target_user: Maintainer of a VitePress documentation project

use_cases.title: Prepare a Static Site Deployment
use_cases.description: Choose build commands, output directories, base paths, cache headers, and hosting configuration for common platforms.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Site
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new VitePress project
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a simple VitePress documentation plan for my project. Include config, pages, navigation, sidebar, and package scripts.

prompt_templates.title: Configure Theme Navigation
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design VitePress themeConfig for these docs sections: [sections]. Include nav, sidebar, edit links, search, and footer.

prompt_templates.title: Add Data-Driven Pages
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a VitePress dynamic route workflow for [content type]. Explain the template, paths loader, watched files, and content payload.

prompt_templates.title: Audit and Deploy Docs
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my VitePress configuration and deployment target: [details]. Identify build, base path, routing, cache, SSR, and hosting issues.

output_examples.input: I need docs for a Vue library with guide and API sections.
output_examples.output: A site plan with VitePress config, navigation, sidebar groups, starter pages, package scripts, and suggested Markdown organization.

output_examples.input: My docs need blog-like pages generated from local Markdown files.
output_examples.output: A dynamic route design that uses a paths loader, watched content files, frontmatter fields, and a template page.

output_examples.input: I want to deploy VitePress to GitHub Pages under a repository path.
output_examples.output: A deployment checklist covering base path, build command, output directory, GitHub Actions workflow, and Pages settings.

best_practices: Check `.vitepress/config.ts` before changing routes, theme settings, or deployment paths.
Keep build-time data small because VitePress serializes loaded data into the client bundle.
Review generated commands and deployment settings before running them in a repository or CI environment.
anti_patterns: Do not copy placeholder API keys, URLs, or organization names into production configuration.
Do not access browser-only APIs at module level in Vue components used by VitePress.
Do not deploy with an incorrect base path for GitHub Pages or sub-path hosting.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps AI agents create, configure, theme, extend, and deploy VitePress documentation sites.

faq.question: Can it build or deploy my site automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples. Your agent or local tools must run builds and deployments separately.

faq.question: Does it support Vue components in Markdown?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for Vue components, script setup, directives, and SSR compatibility in Markdown pages.

faq.question: Can it help with dynamic routes?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains paths loader files, route parameters, watched files, and content payloads for generated pages.

faq.question: Which deployment targets are covered?
faq.answer: It covers GitHub Pages, Netlify, Vercel, Cloudflare Pages, GitLab Pages, Firebase, Nginx, and other static hosts.

faq.question: Is the security audit blocking publication?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed static findings are documentation examples or placeholders, so the skill is safe to publish with low risk.
